title: 10 best herbal tinctures for chickenpox
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for chickenpox are concentrated liquid extracts of herbs, created by soaking plant material in a solvent such as alcohol or vinegar. These tinctures can help alleviate symptoms of chickenpox, such as itching and fever, due to their anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antipyretic properties. Examples of herbs used in tinctures for chickenpox include St. John's Wort, licorice root, and lemon balm. By reducing itching and discomfort, these tinctures can improve a patient's quality of life during the healing process. For instance, St. John's Wort can help soothe the skin and promote healing, while licorice root and lemon balm can help reduce fever and inflammation. As a result, herbal tinctures can provide a natural and effective way to manage chickenpox symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower tincture helps with chickenpox because it contains powerful anti-viral and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's active compounds, such as echinacea purpurea, have been shown to boost the immune system and help the body fight off the chickenpox virus. Additionally, the tincture can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as itching and pain associated with chickenpox. It can also help speed up the healing process of the skin lesions caused by the virus. Overall, eastern purple coneflower tincture can provide relief and support the body's natural defenses during a chickenpox infection.
remedy_properties: Antiviral property: Eastern purple coneflower tincture can help reduce the severity of chickenpox symptoms by inhibiting the spread of the varicella-zoster virus, thanks to the presence of antiviral compounds like polyacetylenes and flavonoids.
Anti-inflammatory property: The tincture can alleviate skin irritation and itching caused by chickenpox, as it contains anti-inflammatory agents such as caffeic acid and chlorogenic acid.
Antipruritic property: Eastern purple coneflower tincture can help soothe the itchiness associated with chickenpox, due to the presence of antipruritic constituents like alkamides.
Immunomodulatory property: The tincture can support the immune system and promote faster recovery from chickenpox, as it contains immunomodulatory compounds like polysaccharides and essential oils.

Antioxidant property: Eastern purple coneflower tincture can help protect the body from oxidative stress and cellular damage caused by chickenpox, thanks to the presence of antioxidant constituents like phenolic acids and flavonoids.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 oz of dried Eastern purple coneflower (Echinacea purpurea) herb into small pieces.
Place herb in a clean 1-quart glass jar and add 1 pint of 100-proof vodka.
Tightly seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks.
Shake the jar gently every few days.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle and store in a cool, dark place. Take 1-2 mL by mouth up to three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild indigo tincture helps with chickenpox because it has powerful anti-viral and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the severity and duration of the symptoms of chickenpox. The active compounds found in wild indigo, such as indigoquinone and isoindigo, have been shown to inhibit the replication of the virus that causes chickenpox, which can help to speed up the healing process. Additionally, wild indigo can help to reduce the redness, swelling, and itching associated with chickenpox, providing relief from the discomfort caused by the infection. By boosting the immune system and reducing inflammation, wild indigo tincture can help to promote a faster recovery from chickenpox.
remedy_properties: Antiviral: Wild indigo tincture contains active constituents like alkaloids and isoflavonoids that inhibit the replication of chickenpox-causing varicella-zoster virus, thereby reducing the severity and duration of the disease.

Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of wild indigo tincture, due to its isoflavonoids and polyphenols, help alleviate skin inflammation, itching, and pain associated with chickenpox skin lesions.

Immunostimulant: Wild indigo tincture enhances the immune system's response to chickenpox by activating immune cells, thanks to its polysaccharides and alkaloids, which helps the body fight off the virus and recover more quickly.
Antipyretic: Wild indigo tincture's antipyretic properties, attributed to its alkaloids and flavonoids, help regulate body temperature, reducing fever and discomfort caused by chickenpox.

Antipruritic: The antipruritic effect of wild indigo tincture, resulting from its alkaloids and tannins, helps relieve itching and scratching caused by chickenpox skin lesions, promoting healing and preventing secondary infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest wild indigo plants, ensuring the above-ground portion is fully grown and in bloom.
Chop the harvested wild indigo into small pieces, filling a clean, dry mason jar up to 3/4 full.
Pour 80-proof alcohol (or a 1:1 ratio of alcohol and distilled water) into the jar, completely covering the plant material and leaving a 1-inch space at the top.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, gently shaking the mixture every few days.
Strain the tincture using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the plant material. Store the tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tincture helps with chickenpox because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The herb contains hypericin and hyperforin compounds, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and inhibit the replication of the virus that causes chickenpox. By taking St. John's wort tincture, the herb's active constituents can help alleviate itching, reduce the number of lesions, and speed up the healing process. Additionally, St. John's wort tincture may help to boost the immune system, which can help the body fight off the virus more effectively. It is important to note that St. John's wort tincture should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment or advice.
remedy_properties: Antiviral property: St. John's wort tincture can help reduce the severity of chickenpox symptoms by inhibiting the spread of the varicella-zoster virus, thanks to the active constituents hypericin and hyperforin.
Anti-inflammatory property: The tincture can alleviate skin irritation and itching caused by chickenpox, as hypericin and flavonoids in the plant have anti-inflammatory effects.

Antipruritic property: St. John's wort tincture can help soothe itchy skin and reduce the desire to scratch, which is important for preventing scarring and limiting the spread of the virus; this is attributed to the active constituents hypericin and pseudohypericin.
Analgesic property: The tincture can help alleviate pain associated with chickenpox, including skin irritation and fever; this is due to the presence of hyperforin and other active compounds.

Antibacterial property: St. John's wort tincture can help prevent secondary bacterial infections that may occur as a result of scratching or broken blisters, as hyperforin and flavonoids exhibit antibacterial properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest St. John's Wort flowers and leaves, ensuring they are dry and free from dew.
Chop or crush 2 ounces of the herb, placing it in a clean, dry glass jar.
Pour 1 pint of 80-proof vodka or rum over the herb, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the tincture using cheesecloth, discarding the plant material, and store the liquid in a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Black cherry tincture helps with chickenpox because it contains anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that can alleviate the symptoms of the virus. The herbal extract has high levels of anthocyanins, which are known to reduce inflammation and pain. Additionally, black cherry tincture can help to boost the immune system, allowing the body to fight off the virus more effectively. The antiviral properties of the tincture can also help to reduce the severity and duration of the chickenpox outbreak. Furthermore, black cherry tincture has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce the itchiness and discomfort associated with chickenpox.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Black cherry tincture helps reduce inflammation caused by chickenpox, easing discomfort and speeding up recovery. This property is due to the presence of anthocyanins and other flavonoids.

Antiviral: The antiviral properties of black cherry tincture can help inhibit the spread and replication of the chickenpox virus. The antiviral activity comes from compounds like anthocyanins and other polyphenols.

Analgesic: The analgesic properties of black cherry tincture help alleviate pain and itching associated with chickenpox. These pain-relieving effects are attributed to the presence of compounds like anthocyanins and other flavonoids.

Antipyretic: Black cherry tincture can help reduce fever caused by chickenpox, promoting a quicker recovery. The antipyretic properties are due to the presence of compounds like anthocyanins and other polyphenols.

Anti-pruritic: Black cherry tincture has anti-pruritic properties that help reduce itching and inflammation associated with chickenpox. This property is due to the presence of anthocyanins and other flavonoids.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 cups of black cherry tree bark into small pieces, place in a clean jar, then add 4 cups of 100-proof vodka.
Tightly seal the jar, shake it well, then store in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks.
Shake the jar vigorously once a day during the 4-6 week period.
After 4-6 weeks, strain the mixture using a cheesecloth into a clean jar, squeezing out the liquid from the bark.
Store the final black cherry tincture in a cool, dark place and take 1-2 mL by mouth up to four times a day for chickenpox treatment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fernleaf biscuitroot tincture helps with chickenpox because it contains antiviral properties that can help reduce the severity and duration of the virus. The herb's active compounds, such as saponins and flavonoids, have been shown to inhibit the replication of the chickenpox virus, thereby reducing the number of skin lesions and preventing further spread of the infection. Additionally, fernleaf biscuitroot has anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which can help alleviate the itching, pain, and discomfort associated with chickenpox. By taking fernleaf biscuitroot tincture at the onset of chickenpox symptoms, individuals may experience a milder and shorter course of the illness.
remedy_properties: Antiviral: Fernleaf biscuitroot tincture has antiviral properties that can help reduce the severity and duration of chickenpox symptoms by inhibiting the spread of the varicella-zoster virus. Active constituents responsible for this property include phenolic acids and flavonoids.

Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of fernleaf biscuitroot tincture can help alleviate itching, swelling, and redness caused by chickenpox, providing relief from discomfort. Key active constituents responsible for this property include steroidal saponins and phenolic acids.

Antipruritic: Fernleaf biscuitroot tincture has antipruritic properties that can help reduce itching and scratching associated with chickenpox, preventing secondary infections and scarring. The active constituents responsible for this property include steroidal saponins and tannins.

Immunomodulatory: Fernleaf biscuitroot tincture has immunomodulatory properties that can help boost the immune system, enabling the body to fight off the chickenpox virus more effectively. Active constituents responsible for this property include polysaccharides and alkaloids.

Adaptogenic: Fernleaf biscuitroot tincture has adaptogenic properties that can help the body cope with the stress and fatigue caused by chickenpox, promoting faster recovery. Key active constituents responsible for this property include steroidal saponins and phenolic acids.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried fernleaf biscuitroot root and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 pint of 100-proof alcohol or glycerin over the chopped root, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture after 2-4 weeks, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Administer 30 drops of the tincture every 3 hours for children with chickenpox, as needed for relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mayapple tincture helps with chickenpox because it contains berberine, a compound with antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. This active ingredient may inhibit the replication of the varicella-zoster virus, which causes chickenpox. Additionally, mayapple tincture can help alleviate symptoms such as itching and irritation associated with the viral infection. The herb's anti-inflammatory effects can reduce redness and swelling, providing relief to individuals suffering from chickenpox. However, it is crucial to consult a healthcare professional before administering mayapple tincture or any other natural remedy to treat chickenpox.
remedy_properties: Antiviral: Mayapple tincture contains berberine, an alkaloid that exhibits antiviral properties, which can help reduce the replication of the varicella-zoster virus that causes chickenpox.
Anti-inflammatory: The presence of steroidal saponins in mayapple tincture can help alleviate skin inflammation and reduce itching caused by chickenpox.
Antipruritic: Mayapple tincture has antipruritic properties, which can help relieve the itching and discomfort associated with chickenpox, thus preventing skin irritation and potential infection.
Immunostimulant: Mayapple tincture can stimulate the immune system, helping the body to better fight off the chickenpox virus and recover more quickly.
Antipyretic: Mayapple tincture can help reduce fever, a common symptom of chickenpox, by acting as an antipyretic, thus providing relief and preventing potential complications.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mayapple roots, ensuring they are at least three years old; clean and chop into small pieces.
Fill a clean jar with chopped mayapple roots, adding 80-proof vodka until the roots are fully submerged.
Securely cover the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for four to six weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth after four to six weeks, discarding the mayapple roots.
Store the finished mayapple tincture in a dark glass bottle, using 10-15 drops up to three times a day for chickenpox treatment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elderberry tincture helps with chickenpox because it is rich in immune-boosting properties that can help reduce the severity and duration of the virus. The powerful antiviral and anti-inflammatory compounds found in elderberries can help alleviate the itching, pain, and inflammation associated with chickenpox. Additionally, elderberries are packed with antioxidants that can help strengthen the immune system and promote faster healing. By taking elderberry tincture at the onset of chickenpox symptoms, you can potentially reduce the number of chickenpox lesions and shorten the duration of the illness.
remedy_properties: Antiviral: Elderberry tincture inhibits the replication of the chickenpox virus, helping to reduce the severity and duration of the illness. This property is important because it directly targets the cause of chickenpox, providing symptomatic relief. Active constituents include anthocyanins and flavonoids.

Anti-inflammatory: Elderberry tincture reduces inflammation and swelling, alleviating discomfort caused by skin lesions and itching. This property is important because it minimizes the distressing symptoms associated with chickenpox. Active constituents include flavonoids and triterpenes.

Immune-boosting: Elderberry tincture strengthens the immune system, enhancing the body's ability to fight off the chickenpox virus. This property is important because it promotes overall well-being and accelerates recovery. Active constituents include anthocyanins and vitamins A, C, and E.

Diaphoretic: Elderberry tincture promotes sweating, helping to eliminate toxins and reduce fever. This property is important because it supports the body's natural detoxification process and assists in regulating body temperature during chickenpox. Active constituents include flavonoids and tannins.

Antioxidant: Elderberry tincture neutralizes free radicals, protecting cells from damage and supporting the immune system. This property is important because it helps maintain the body's overall health and resilience during chickenpox. Active constituents include anthocyanins and flavonoids.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 cups of elderberries, placing them in a clean jar.
Pour 1 quart of vodka or rum over the elderberries, filling the jar to cover the berries.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth, discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in a tightly sealed glass container at room temperature for up to a year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tincture helps with chickenpox because it contains anti-viral, anti-inflammatory, and immune-boosting properties that can alleviate symptoms and accelerate recovery. The herb's active compounds, such as taraxacum and sesquiterpene lactones, inhibit the chickenpox virus from replicating and spreading, while reducing inflammation and redness in the skin. Moreover, dandelion stimulates the immune system, enabling the body to combat the virus more effectively. As a result, dandelion tincture can help soothe itchiness, prevent infection, and shorten the duration of chickenpox.
remedy_properties: Anti-pruritic: Dandelion tincture can help alleviate itching associated with chickenpox, reducing the urge to scratch and lowering the risk of scarring. This property is due to the presence of tannins and essential oils in dandelion.

Anti-inflammatory: Dandelion tincture helps reduce inflammation and redness caused by chickenpox, providing relief from discomfort and promoting healing. The anti-inflammatory effect is attributed to the flavonoids and triterpenes found in dandelion.

Antimicrobial: Dandelion tincture has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent secondary bacterial infections during chickenpox, contributing to overall recovery. The antimicrobial properties are attributed to the sesquiterpene lactones and phenolic acids present in dandelion.

Immunomodulatory: Dandelion tincture supports the immune system, helping the body fight off the chickenpox virus and recover more quickly. The immunomodulatory effects are due to the presence of polysaccharides and vitamins A, C, and D in dandelion.

Diaphoretic: Dandelion tincture promotes sweating, which aids in detoxification and helps bring down fever during chickenpox, supporting overall well-being. The diaphoretic effect is attributed to the bitter compounds and volatile oils found in dandelion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dandelion plants, choosing flowers, leaves, and roots from organic, pesticide-free areas; rinse and air-dry.
Coarsely chop dandelion plant material, filling a clean, dry glass jar up to three-quarters full.
Pour 80-proof or higher food-grade alcohol (like vodka or brandy) into the jar, ensuring all plant material is submerged; cap the jar.
Store the jar in a cool, dark location for four to six weeks, gently shaking the mixture every few days.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, squeezing out the liquid; store the tincture in a dark glass bottle, refrigerating for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tincture helps with chickenpox because it contains anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antimicrobial properties that can help reduce inflammation, fight off the virus, and prevent infection. The herb's active compounds, such as allantoin, aid in the healing of skin lesions, while its anti-pruritic properties help alleviate itching and discomfort. Moreover, plantain tincture's demulcent qualities soothe irritated skin, providing relief from the symptoms of chickenpox. Its immune-boosting properties also help strengthen the body's defenses against the virus, promoting a faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain tincture helps reduce inflammation and itching caused by chickenpox, soothing irritated skin and promoting faster healing. This property is due to the presence of flavonoids, tannins, and mucilage in plantain.

Antiviral: Plantain tincture has antiviral properties that can help inhibit the spread and replication of the chickenpox virus, reducing the severity and duration of the infection. These antiviral effects are attributed to the plant's polyphenols and flavonoids.

Antipruritic: Plantain tincture has antipruritic properties, which help alleviate itching and scratching associated with chickenpox. This property is particularly helpful in reducing the risk of scarring and secondary infections. The antipruritic effect is mainly due to the presence of iridoids and tannins in plantain.

Astringent: Plantain tincture has astringent properties that help dry up oozing blisters and promote skin regeneration. This aids in the formation of new skin cells and speeds up the healing process. The astringent properties of plantain are due to the presence of tannins.

Demulcent: Plantain tincture has demulcent properties that help soothe and protect the skin, providing a protective barrier against external irritants and infections. This property helps maintain skin integrity and supports the overall healing process. The demulcent effect is primarily due to the presence of mucilage in plantain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest two cups of fresh plantain leaves, rinse, and chop coarsely.
Combine leaves and one pint of 100-proof vodka in a quart jar, ensuring leaves are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, discarding plant material, and store tincture in a dark glass bottle.
Administer 10-15 drops of tincture orally, three times a day, for chickenpox treatment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tincture helps with chickenpox because it has anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antipruritic properties that can alleviate symptoms of the disease. The herb's active compounds, such as alkaloids, flavonoids, and tannins, work together to reduce inflammation, fight the virus, and soothe itchiness. Yarrow tincture can also promote wound healing and prevent scarring, making it an ideal remedy for chickenpox-related skin lesions. Moreover, yarrow has been traditionally used to boost the immune system, which can help the body fight off the virus more effectively. Therefore, yarrow tincture can provide relief and comfort to those suffering from chickenpox.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tincture's anti-inflammatory property, due to the presence of flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, helps reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with chickenpox blisters.

Antimicrobial: Yarrow tincture's antimicrobial property, attributed to the presence of volatile oils like azulene and camphor, helps prevent secondary bacterial infections during the chickenpox healing process.
Antipyretic: Yarrow tincture's antipyretic property, owing to the presence of alkamides, aids in reducing fever and discomfort caused by chickenpox.
Analgesic: Yarrow tincture's analgesic property, due to the presence of lactones and flavonoids, helps alleviate pain and discomfort associated with chickenpox symptoms.
Astringent: Yarrow tincture's astringent property, owing to the presence of tannins, helps dry out and heal chickenpox blisters, promoting faster recovery.
remedy_recipe: Chop fresh yarrow flowers and leaves, filling a clean, dry mason jar up to 3/4 full.
Pour 100-proof vodka into the jar, covering the yarrow and leaving a 1-inch space at the top.
Seal the jar tightly and shake it vigorously for 1-2 minutes.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle, discarding the yarrow solids.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat chickenpox with herbal tinctures is by using a combination of anti-viral and anti-inflammatory herbs. St. John's Wort and Licorice Root are known for their antiviral properties, helping to reduce the severity and duration of the virus. Meanwhile, Calendula and Chamomile have anti-inflammatory effects, soothing the itchiness and irritation of the skin lesions. Always consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new treatment.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to chickenpox that are treated with herbal tinctures are shingles and some skin rashes. For shingles, tinctures made from St. John's Wort, licorice root, or lemon balm may be used. These herbs have antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and inflammation. For skin rashes, tinctures made from calendula, chamomile, or plantain can be used. These herbs have soothing and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce itching and irritation. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any herbal treatment.
